translationGuidelines: Start from the mapcir skeleton but keep package and module names aligned to Foodike.
Do not carry Android dependency concepts into Cangjie build config except as replacement notes.
Preserve the source bundle/application identity and startup label resources.
Map the Android launcher activity and splash theme into HarmonyOS entry-module metadata.
Keep ability code thin and compose startup/session services instead of storing repository logic here.
Preserve startup destination selection, shared shell ownership, and conditional bottom-bar/cart-button display.
Keep immutable business models separate from feature stores and resource adaptation helpers.
Split the very large source fake-data file by target responsibility but keep the content semantics and identifiers stable enough for feature parity.
This is the primary persisted state boundary and should use a HarmonyOS-direct preference API rather than in-memory fallback.
Keep session-scoped mutable state in one repository boundary, but avoid folding it into the UI shell.
Preserve repository method boundaries because multiple features depend on them for screen state composition.
Preserve the source startup contract: route is resolved before the shell becomes interactive.
Keep field state, validation, persisted login mutation, and snackbar/error emission separate from page widgets.
Preserve the source ordering of operations when a restaurant is selected: update repository/session first, then route to detail.
Keep detail mutation logic explicit so floating-cart visibility and section expansion remain easy to verify.
Keep cart snapshot loading separate from UI-local coupon/note editor state.

rules: Migrate all 52 source strings before final feature verification.
Retain visible labels and content descriptions even when source logic is placeholder-only.
Map splash, page background, primary green, and neutral chrome colors into HarmonyOS tokens.
Do not leave Android theme names as direct target resource keys if clearer HarmonyOS naming is available.
Copy bitmap assets directly when compatible.
Convert Android vectors and adaptive-icon resources into HarmonyOS media assets or equivalent target resource composition.
Keep card, onboarding, profile, rating, and splash visuals source-identifiable.
Preserve launcher identity, startup entry binding, and page profile references.

dependencies.targetStrategy: Replace with HarmonyOS Cangjie ArkUI components and feature-specific builder composition.
Replace with an app router plus page stack coordination in Cangjie; keep explicit route ids and shell rules.
Replace with a direct HarmonyOS preference or app-storage service callable from Cangjie.
Use HarmonyOS startup-page behavior or platform-replaced startup gating while preserving delayed initial route reveal.
dependencies.targetDependency: HarmonyOS startup window resources and page lifecycle

dependencies.targetStrategy: Replace with simple explicit service construction and composition roots in the target app shell; avoid introducing a DI framework unless required later.
Replace with native HarmonyOS pager/list layout patterns or a verified Cangjie-callable package only if needed.

tests.name: source-example-unit-test
tests.reason: Boilerplate arithmetic test does not verify app behavior.

tests.name: source-example-instrumented-test
tests.reason: Boilerplate package-name assertion does not verify translated behavior.
targetTests: basic target build/test harness smoke only if the template supports it

tests.name: startup-session-tests
tests.reason: Critical startup destination and persisted login behavior are not covered by source tests.

tests.name: login-validation-tests
tests.reason: Credential validation and snackbar emission are user-visible and currently untested.

tests.name: selection-and-detail-state-tests
tests.reason: Restaurant selection ordering and detail expansion state are high-risk behavior paths.

tests.name: pure-helper-tests
tests.reason: Small helper modules can provide stable automated coverage even if UI automation is limited.

phases.title: Project skeleton and resources
targetScope: Bootstrap Foodike-Harmony from the mapcir skeleton.
Create entry/AppScope configs, package roots, base resources, and domain/data skeletons.
Lay down target file structure for feature stores, repositories, and page builders.
expectedTests: No feature tests required yet; optional compile-only smoke after skeleton bootstrap.
stateDataBehavior: Resource keys for strings, colors, icons, and startup visuals must exist before feature pages reference them.
Domain models and repositories stay separate so later phases do not collapse business logic into the shell.
deferredRisks: If the skeleton package root is not normalized early, later matrix endpoint validation will become noisy.
Leaving large resource gaps beyond phase 1 will block reliable page composition in later phases.

implementationGroups.title: Bootstrap target entry module
uiContracts: single entry ability launching the app shell
dataContracts: none in phase 1 beyond package/module wiring
requiredBehaviors: Target project exists with a valid entry module and correct root package.
App metadata aligns with source identity and startup resources.
riskHints: Do not leave the target in a rootless or placeholder package state.
Keep ability shell thin so later logic lands in domain features instead of the entry file.
acceptanceEvidence: Build gate passes for phase-01-project-skeleton-resources.
entry/cjpm.toml and module metadata exist under Foodike-Harmony.

implementationGroups.title: Migrate strings, colors, and media
uiContracts: onboarding/login/home/history/detail/cart/profile labels
startup icon and splash visuals
requiredBehaviors: All source strings have target resource keys.
Referenced card, icon, and onboarding media are available in target resources.
riskHints: Resource shrinkage is currently total; do not postpone the base catalog beyond phase 1.
Vector and adaptive-icon assets may need conversion instead of direct copying.
acceptanceEvidence: Target string and media resources exist and are referenced by planned target pages.
No phase-1 code references missing resource keys.

phases.title: Startup, navigation, and session services
targetScope: Implement persisted login state service and startup route resolution.
Create target routing model, app shell, and shared bottom-bar/cart-button visibility behavior.
Introduce session repository boundaries for selected restaurant, favourites, menu items, and cart snapshots.
stateDataBehavior: Persisted login state must control the first route before the app shell becomes interactive.
Bottom bar appears only on home/history routes when the shared top-level scroll state is at the first item.
Floating cart action remains shell-owned, not duplicated by feature pages.
deferredRisks: If startup routing is made asynchronous without a guard screen, users may briefly see the wrong page.
Session repository boundaries must stay explicit or later detail/home/history parity will be hard to verify.

implementationGroups.title: Startup routing and persisted login state
uiContracts: startup gate before page routing
logged-in users land on home
logged-out users land on onboarding
dataContracts: boolean login state persisted across launches
requiredBehaviors: Initial route depends on persisted login flag.
Login and logout both mutate the same persisted state service.
riskHints: Do not replace persistence with in-memory state.
Avoid blocking initialization unless the target framework requires it.
acceptanceEvidence: Build gate passes for phase-02-startup-navigation-session.
Target tests prove both login-state values resolve to the correct first route.

implementationGroups.title: App shell and route composition
uiContracts: onboarding/login/home/history/cart/profile/detail routes
bottom bar visible only on home/history when at top
floating cart action above bottom shell
dataContracts: shared top-level scroll state influences shell visibility
requiredBehaviors: Route stack preserves source destination set.
State restoration or equivalent non-duplicating navigation semantics are retained for shell tabs.
riskHints: Do not collapse all feature pages into one giant page file.
Treat the duplicate onboarding composable in source as analyzer noise, not a second real feature.
acceptanceEvidence: Target shell renders route-specific chrome correctly.
Home/history tab switching does not stack duplicate pages.

phases.title: Onboarding, login, and profile flows
targetScope: Implement login form state, credential validation, snackbar errors, and successful navigation.
Implement profile view and logout flow.
stateDataBehavior: Onboarding advances page by page and finishes by routing to login.
Login state updates email/password immediately and emits snackbar errors on invalid credentials.
Logout flips the same persisted flag used by startup routing and clears navigation history back to onboarding.
deferredRisks: Social login, sign-up, forgot-password, and profile-edit actions are placeholders in source and should remain visibly present or explicitly replaced, not removed.

implementationGroups.title: Onboarding pager and progress flow
uiContracts: three onboarding pages
progress indicators
next action advances page or routes to login
requiredBehaviors: Pager state is preserved within the onboarding session.
Final step transitions to login only after the last page.
riskHints: Accompanist pager is source-specific; do not copy its API shape into the target.
acceptanceEvidence: Page indicators and next button behavior match source progression semantics.

implementationGroups.title: Login and logout session flows
uiContracts: invalid login shows snackbar
valid login routes to home and clears back stack
logout routes to onboarding and clears back stack
dataContracts: login flag toggles through the persisted session service
requiredBehaviors: Credential entry updates local field state immediately.
Incorrect credentials keep the user on the login page with an error message.
Login and logout both mutate the persisted login state service.
riskHints: Do not replace snackbar feedback with silent failure.
Do not infer real authentication or network behavior that does not exist in source.
acceptanceEvidence: Target tests verify successful and unsuccessful login outcomes.
Build gate passes for phase-03-onboarding-login-profile.

phases.title: Home and history discovery flows
targetScope: Implement fixture-backed home and history pages.
Implement shared restaurant selection commands and local discovery UI state.
Keep top section, search, chip filters, ads, recommended cards, favourites, and history tabs modular.
stateDataBehavior: Home loads three static content groups plus a live favourites snapshot from the session repository.
History reads the same favourites snapshot and uses tab-local UI state.
Search text and chip selection update immediately as local UI state even though source does not apply repository filtering.
deferredRisks: Source filters and search currently stop at local state; target should not invent data-filtering semantics without explicit justification.

implementationGroups.title: Home discovery content and restaurant selection
uiContracts: home top section routes to profile
favourites render only when non-empty
restaurant tap stores selection then routes to detail
search field state updates immediately
chip selection toggles immediately
dataContracts: ads, food items, restaurants, and favourites are read from repositories
requiredBehaviors: Home sections appear in the same source order.
A selected restaurant is saved before detail navigation occurs.
riskHints: Do not push home-specific state into the global shell.
Search and filter controls are visible behavior even without backend filtering.
acceptanceEvidence: Target tests verify selection command ordering.
Build gate passes for phase-04-home-history-discovery.

implementationGroups.title: History tabs and favourites reuse
uiContracts: history page shows search bar and tabs
favourites tab uses liked-restaurant data
restaurant tap stores selection then routes to detail
dataContracts: liked restaurants come from the same session repository as home
requiredBehaviors: History preserves tab-local state and list rendering semantics.
Selection command matches home/detail flow.
riskHints: The source history section is largely placeholder content; keep visible structure without inventing backend behavior.
acceptanceEvidence: Target history store test proves selection command behavior.
Page build shows tab sections with the expected labels and ordering.

phases.title: Detail and cart state mutations
targetScope: Implement detail-state composition from the selected restaurant and session snapshots.
Implement local expansion, like, quantity, and cart-visibility behavior.
Implement cart page rendering with item-note, coupon, delivery, and bill sections.
stateDataBehavior: Recommended, non-veg, and veg sections derive from current menu snapshots and preserve independent expansion toggles.
Like toggle updates immediately in local detail state.
Menu-item add/subtract controls update visible counts immediately in-place.
Floating cart action appears only when the visible detail item total is non-zero.
Cart page filters out zero-count items and keeps note/coupon text local to the page.
deferredRisks: The source never persists detail quantity changes back into repository cart data, creating a parity tension between visible local counts and cart snapshot data.
If target harmonizes this inconsistency, the later matrix record may need an explicit simplified note.

implementationGroups.title: Restaurant detail page and menu-section state
uiContracts: back, like, and share actions in the header
three expandable menu sections with animated arrow rotation
floating cart action only when item counts are non-zero
dataContracts: selected restaurant comes from session repository
menu and liked state come from detail store composition
requiredBehaviors: Section toggles update immediately and independently.
Like toggle updates immediately.
Menu-item counts update immediately in visible UI state.
riskHints: Do not hide the cart action behind repository-only counts because the source uses local visible counts.
Share is a visible affordance but currently no-op in source; preserve the affordance even if still non-functional.
acceptanceEvidence: Detail store test verifies expansion and cart-visibility logic.
Build gate passes for phase-05-detail-cart-state-mutations.

implementationGroups.title: Cart page sections and local editor states
uiContracts: cart list hides zero-count items
empty cart shows empty message
item-note editor and coupon field update locally
delivery and bill sections remain visible below items
dataContracts: cart snapshot comes from session repository
delivery restaurant and bill total are currently hard-coded UI content in source
requiredBehaviors: Cart renders snapshot data in the same order as source sections.
Note/coupon field edits update immediately without requiring repository round-trips.
riskHints: Avoid inventing checkout or persistence flows that do not exist in source.
Bill and delivery are source placeholders; keep them visible but do not over-model them as real backend state.
acceptanceEvidence: Cart page composes item, coupon, delivery, and bill sections with the expected visible conditions.

phases.title: Polish, tests, and verification
targetScope: Add and run focused target tests when the template allows it.
Prepare evidence for later narrow coverage and capability backfill sessions.
stateDataBehavior: Target tests focus on pure behavior and store logic so they remain stable without fragile UI automation.
No resource key used by code should remain unresolved.
deferredRisks: If the target template blocks runnable tests, verification evidence will rely more heavily on direct endpoint review and build gates until the harness is repaired.

implementationGroups.title: Target-side verification harness
uiContracts: none; phase focuses on store-level behavior validation
requiredBehaviors: Critical business and state logic have target-side automated coverage where feasible.
riskHints: Do not use generated placeholder tests as the only proof of behavior.
Record exact test harness blockers if `cjpm test` cannot run.
acceptanceEvidence: `cjpm test` passes, or the exact blocker is recorded with compile/build evidence for the tested modules.

implementationGroups.title: Resource closeout and evidence prep
resourceInputs: all source drawables and mipmaps used by the translated pages
uiContracts: every visible label and icon reference used by translated pages resolves in target resources
requiredBehaviors: No translated feature depends on missing resource keys.
Plan artifacts are ready for later plannedPhase backfill and matrix evidence updates.
riskHints: Resource omissions can hide as late compile or runtime defects if not closed here.
acceptanceEvidence: Final build gate passes and resource coverage warnings are either resolved or explicitly deferred with reason.

deferred.title: Detail quantity state versus cart snapshot consistency
deferred.reason: The source app keeps menu-item quantity state locally inside `MenuItemCard` while the cart page reads a separate repository snapshot. Preserving visible behavior is straightforward, but a fully unified target state model may exceed source fidelity unless handled carefully.
deferred.targetFallback: Preserve immediate local quantity updates and cart-button visibility first, while keeping explicit store boundaries so later reconciliation is possible.
deferred.risk: If target unifies the states without care, cart behavior may diverge from source. If target keeps them separate, coverage records may need an explicit simplified rationale.
validationNeeded: manual comparison of detail quantity interactions versus cart rendering
store-level test coverage for detail visible counts
deferred.futureCompletionPath: After feature implementation, decide whether exact source inconsistency or a documented simplified reconciliation best preserves user-visible behavior, then reflect that decision in coverage records.

deferred.title: Capability and matrix cleanup for heuristic false positives
deferred.reason: Generated planning evidence still marks several files with media, SAF, runtime-permission, and notification/widget concerns that source walkthrough evidence does not support as real behaviors.
deferred.targetFallback: Do not schedule these capabilities for implementation work unless later source inspection finds real evidence.
deferred.risk: If row-level backfill happens before the walkthrough evidence is applied, later sessions may chase non-existent capabilities.
validationNeeded: narrow post-planning matrix and capability review using exact authority patch commands
deferred.futureCompletionPath: Use the later records-only planning backfill session to apply row-level plannedPhase and notes updates based on the walkthrough overrides.

deferred.title: Runnable target test harness availability
deferred.reason: The target project is currently empty, and no verification results exist yet. Runnable `cjpm test` support depends on the final HarmonyOS template and module wiring that phase 1 must establish.
deferred.targetFallback: Keep critical logic in small pure modules so compile/build evidence remains meaningful if the runtime test harness needs extra setup.
deferred.risk: Without runnable tests, feature evidence may rely too heavily on build-only signals unless store logic remains easy to inspect directly.
validationNeeded: record exact blocker if the harness still cannot run
deferred.futureCompletionPath: If `cjpm test` is blocked, repair the target test wiring in phase 6 and update verification evidence with the exact command outcome.

analysisGaps.impact: Planning relied on primary Kotlin analysis, Android screen map, behavior spec, and direct source inspection instead of these missing summaries.
The plan was built from protocol rules and direct evidence rather than a prior plan-contract validator report.

sourceOverrides.override: Treat media-library, SAF/file access, runtime-permission, and notification/widget tags as reviewed noise for this app unless later source inspection contradicts the walkthrough.
Source search and filter controls currently maintain local visible state only; target should preserve their immediate UI behavior without inventing repository filtering logic.
